summary: date-fns-lite postinstall exfiltrates SSH keys, cloud/CI tokens, and probes for container escape
details: The package presents itself as a lightweight date formatting utility and impersonates the popular date-fns library, but its postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and performs installer-side credential theft. postinstall.js reads ~/.ssh (including id_rsa and authorized_keys, additionally attempting /proc/1/root/root/.ssh/* when running as root), ~/.gitconfig, ~/.npmrc, cmdkey stored credentials, and browser profile data (Chrome/Edge bookmarks, history, and login data paths), and enumerates a hardcoded list of sensitive environment variables including AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET, GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, and DOCKER_PASSWORD. The collected data, along with hostname/username system info, is JSON-encoded and sent via http.request POST to a hardcoded remote endpoint at 115.190.124.243:9082/callback. In addition, the script attempts Linux container/namespace escape techniques from the install context: reading /proc/1/root/, invoking 'docker run --rm -v /:/mnt alpine ls /mnt' against /var/run/docker.sock, calling 'unshare --mount --fork --pid', and enumerating SUID binaries via 'find / -perm -4000' — an offensive attempt to pivot from a build/CI container to the underlying host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
